title: chamaecyparis boursieri var. alba-spica
intro: Chamaecyparis boursieri var. alba-spica, also known as the 'Taiwan White Fir', is a variety of evergreen coniferous tree that belongs to the cypress family. The plant's medicinal properties lie in its essential oil, which contains compounds like borneol and bornyl acetate, possessing anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties. The health benefits of this plant include its potential to alleviate respiratory issues, reduce inflammation, and aid in wound healing. Herbal preparations of Chamaecyparis boursieri var. alba-spica include essential oil, which can be used topically or added to aromatherapy blends, as well as extracts that can be used in traditional medicine.
